title: Paris Saint-Germain Eliminated From Champions League in Stunning Upset
summary: Benfica's disciplined defensive display and a clinical counter-attacking performance send PSG crashing out at the quarter-final stage.
content:
Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League campaign ended in humiliating fashion on Tuesday night as Benfica produced one of the competition's great upsets, eliminating the Ligue 1 giants with a 2-0 victory at the Parc des Princes that completed a 3-1 aggregate triumph for the Portuguese champions and sent the Parisian club crashing out of the quarter-finals for the third time in five seasons.

The result leaves PSG without a Champions League trophy despite years of transformative investment and signals, many observers believe, that the club must undertake another fundamental reassessment of its sporting project. The defeat comes two years after the club's last major European embarrassment and raises renewed questions about whether extraordinary financial resources alone can build a genuine Champions League winning culture.

Benfica's performance was tactically disciplined and ruthlessly efficient. Manager Roger Schmidt set his side up to absorb PSG's possession and strike on the counter, a game plan that was executed with precision by a squad whose collective work rate and organisational discipline contrasted sharply with PSG's increasingly frustrated and disjointed attacking play.

Goals from Fredrik Aursnes in the 34th minute and a clinical finish from Vangelis Pavlidis in stoppage time sealed the tie. PSG's star-studded forward line was kept largely quiet throughout, with the visitors' back four winning their individual duels with a composure that belied their underdog status going into the match.

PSG president Nasser Al-Khelaifi left the stadium without speaking to the media. Sources within the club indicated that an emergency board meeting had been convened for Thursday to discuss the sporting directorship and coaching staff's positions ahead of what promises to be a turbulent summer transfer window.

title: Dhaka Premier League: Abahani Extend Lead With Dominant Derby Victory
summary: Two goals from striker Suman Reza and a commanding defensive display give Abahani a five-point cushion at the top of the table.
content:
Abahani Limited extended their lead at the top of the Bangladesh Football Premier League to five points with a commanding 3-1 victory over city rivals Mohammedan Sporting Club in a fiercely contested Dhaka derby at the Bangabandhu National Stadium on Sunday afternoon, in front of a crowd of over 25,000 supporters who generated an atmosphere rarely seen in domestic Bangladeshi football.

Striker Suman Reza was the hero of the afternoon, scoring twice in the first half to give Abahani a comfortable cushion before the interval. His first goal, a precise header from a Rakib Hossain cross in the 17th minute, was followed by a brilliant individual effort in the 38th minute when he collected a pass on the edge of the area, turned his marker, and drove a low shot into the far corner with his weaker right foot.

Mohammedan pulled a goal back through Brazilian import Marcos Paulo shortly after the restart, and the match entered an intense period in which either side might have altered the scoreline. But Abahani's Argentine midfielder Diego Saavedra settled the contest with a composed finish from the penalty spot in the 76th minute after a handball by the Mohammedan centre-back that the referee did not hesitate to award despite vehement protests from the visiting players.

The result leaves Abahani in a strong position to claim their first Premier League title in four years, with nine matches remaining. Head coach Paul Put described the performance as one of the team's best of the season and praised the crowd's support. "This is what football should look like in Bangladesh," he said in his post-match press conference. "The fans gave us wings today."

Mohammedan remain in third place and face an increasingly difficult path to the title, with second-placed Sheikh Russel Krira Chakra maintaining their challenge with a 2-0 win over Rahmatganj on the same evening, leaving the top three separated by just six points with the season entering its decisive phase.

title: Bangladesh Women's Cricket Team Wins Asia Cup for First Time
summary: A stunning final over finish against India gives Bangladesh their maiden Asia Cup title and a place in the ICC Women's Championship.
content:
Bangladesh's women's cricket team won the Asia Cup for the first time in their history on Saturday, defeating India by three wickets in a breathtaking final at the R. Premadasa Stadium in Colombo that was decided in the last over and sparked scenes of jubilation among the Bangladesh players and their supporters that will remain etched in the memory of everyone who witnessed it.

Chasing 147 to win, Bangladesh needed 14 runs from the final over with three wickets in hand. Captain Nigar Sultana, batting with extraordinary composure under almost unbearable pressure, struck two successive boundaries and a crucial single to bring the equation down to three needed from two balls before sealing the victory with a flick off her pads that raced to the midwicket boundary for the winning runs.

The scenes that followed — Nigar raising her bat to the sky, teammates sprinting from the dugout, the Bangladesh coaching staff embracing on the boundary — were a fitting culmination to a tournament in which the Tigers had grown in confidence and capability with each successive match, overcoming Sri Lanka, Pakistan, and Thailand in the group stage before defeating a formidable Indian side in a semi-final that many had expected Bangladesh to lose.

Pace bowler Marufa Akter was named player of the tournament for a series of match-winning spells that revealed a young athlete of genuine international class. Her performance in the final, four overs for 22 runs including the crucial wickets of Smriti Mandhana and Harmanpreet Kaur, underlined why Bangladesh women's cricket has reasons for optimism that extend well beyond the boundary rope.

The Asia Cup triumph qualifies Bangladesh for the ICC Women's Championship for the first time, giving them the opportunity to compete against the top-ranked nations in the world in the qualification cycle for the next Women's World Cup. For a programme that was still finding its feet just five years ago, it represents an achievement that the sport's administrators and supporters could barely have dared to imagine.
